trivia_bullets.text: Raucous Carnival is the red/white member of Duskmourn's ten-card common dual-land cycle (the 'unlucky lands' / 'life-check lands'). All ten enter tapped unless any player has 13 or less life and produce two colors of mana. The other nine are Abandoned Campground, Bleeding Woods, Etched Cornfield, Lakeside Shack, Murky Sewer, Neglected Manor, Peculiar Lighthouse, Razortrap Gorge, and Strangled Cemetery. [MTG Wiki: Unlucky land / CoolStuffInc preview article, via WebSearch snippets]
sources: CoolStuffInc (Paige Smith DSK dual lands preview)

trivia_bullets.text: The 13-life trigger is a triskaidekaphobia gag baked into Duskmourn's horror-set design language; community nicknames include 'unlucky lands' and 'life-check lands'. [MTG Wiki: Unlucky land, via WebSearch snippet]
Art by Josu Solano. Common rarity. Released 2024-09-27 as part of Duskmourn: House of Horror (set code DSK). [Scryfall]

Flavor text: 'They say that those who enter the amusement park never come back, and their disembodied laughter joins the others on the wind.' — written in the urban-legend register the unlucky-lands cycle uses across the board. [Scryfall]

Oracle text: 'This land enters tapped unless a player has 13 or less life. {T}: Add {R} or {W}.' Note the trigger reads 'a player' not 'an opponent' — your own life total counts, so paying life or self-damage can untap your own land. [Scryfall / MTG Rocks set review, via WebSearch snippet]

The cycle is the first time common dual lands have a real chance to enter untapped, which raised significant interest in Pauper specifically — Commander uptake is muted because 40-life starts make 13-life-or-less most-of-game a rare condition. [MTG Rocks / EDHREC DSK set review, via WebSearch snippets]

Community sentiment via MTG Rocks coverage: the unlucky-lands cycle was widely received as a strong format-impact pickup for Standard / Modern / Pauper because of the untap condition; received as flavorful for Duskmourn but mechanically odd for Commander. [MTG Rocks: 'New Common Duskmourn Dual Lands Have Multi-Format Potential', via WebSearch snippet]

related_cards.name: Abandoned Campground
related_cards.note: no. 255 — same unlucky-lands cycle (white/black per cycle structure)
